skill.name: minecraft-bukkit-pro
skill.description: Develop Minecraft server plugins with Bukkit, Spigot, and Paper APIs, including event listeners, commands, configuration, integrations, and performance guidance.
skill.summary: Build and improve Minecraft Bukkit, Spigot, and Paper plugins with practical architecture and testing guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance were reviewed against SKILL.md and are false positives from prose or Markdown formatting. The skill does instruct agents to use WebSearch and WebFetch for current API research, so it has a low privacy risk if private project details are included in search queries.

low_findings.title: External Command Finding Is Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The static command execution finding at line 27 is a Markdown inline code reference to a resource path, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. No executable command or command interpolation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 27 only tells the agent to open a local resource file when detailed examples are needed. There is no shell syntax beyond Markdown backticks.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text Substring Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to ordinary documentation words in the skill description and guidance, such as architecture, design, document, and guides. No cryptographic algorithm selection, hashing operation, encryption code, or security implementation appears on these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are descriptive skill instructions rather than source code. They contain no MD5, DES, RC4, SHA1, or other weak algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Finding Is Data Access Guidance
low_findings.description: The static network reconnaissance finding at line 101 refers to a repository pattern for application data access. It does not instruct port scanning, host discovery, vulnerability probing, or other reconnaissance activity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is software architecture for Minecraft plugins. No network scanning command, target, or reconnaissance workflow is present.

low_findings.title: External Research Tools May Expose Private Context
low_findings.description: The skill directs agents to use WebSearch and WebFetch for current practices and API changes. This is legitimate for fast-moving Minecraft server APIs, but users should avoid sending private code, secrets, or server details in search queries.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly asks for web research, which is a real network behavior. It appears intended for documentation lookup, not data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Minecraft plugin development requires careful API choices, event handling, and server performance planning. This skill guides Bukkit, Spigot, and Paper plugin work with practical patterns, testing advice, and version-aware recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Plans Bukkit, Spigot, and Paper plugin architecture.
Designs event listeners, commands, tab completion, and configuration flows.
Recommends performance practices for hot events, chunk loading, and async work.
Guides integrations with Vault, PlaceholderAPI, ProtocolLib, databases, and web APIs.
Explains testing strategies with MockBukkit and real server integration checks.
Advises on cross-version compatibility using modern APIs and fallbacks.
limitations: It does not include source code templates or a runnable plugin project.
It depends on current documentation for exact Paper and Spigot API changes.
It cannot verify behavior on a live Minecraft server by itself.
NMS and packet guidance may require version-specific manual review.

use_cases.title: Create a Gameplay Plugin
use_cases.description: Plan events, commands, permissions, configuration, and persistence for a new server feature.

use_cases.title: Modernize a Legacy Plugin
use_cases.description: Review older Bukkit or Spigot code and identify safer Paper APIs, compatibility layers, and migration steps.

use_cases.title: Improve Server Performance
use_cases.description: Find expensive listeners, blocking operations, and chunk loading risks before they affect players.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Plugin
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a Minecraft Paper plugin for [feature]. Include commands, permissions, events, configuration, and a simple testing checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Review Event Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Bukkit event listener design for [behavior]. Focus on event priority, cancellation, player safety, and server tick impact.

prompt_templates.title: Design Cross-Version Support
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a compatibility approach for a plugin that supports [versions]. Compare Bukkit, Spigot, Paper, NMS, and reflection tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Production Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this plugin architecture for production performance risks. Focus on async I/O, database pooling, chunk loading, hot events, and profiling steps.

output_examples.input: I need a plugin that rewards players for mining rare blocks.
output_examples.output: A feature plan with relevant block events, permission nodes, reward rules, configuration sections, anti-abuse checks, and test scenarios.

output_examples.input: My PlayerMoveEvent listener causes lag on busy servers.
output_examples.output: A performance review that explains event frequency, early returns, region checks, caching, profiling, and safer alternatives.

output_examples.input: I want to support both Spigot and Paper.
output_examples.output: A compatibility strategy that separates shared logic, optional Paper features, build dependencies, runtime detection, and fallback behavior.

best_practices: State target Minecraft versions, server platform, Java version, and dependency constraints before asking for implementation guidance.
Ask for performance and thread-safety review when working with events, databases, files, or network integrations.
Use web research for public API changes, but keep private server details and secrets out of search queries.
anti_patterns: Do not run blocking database, file, or web requests on the main server thread.
Do not rely on NMS or reflection when stable Bukkit or Paper APIs solve the same problem.
Do not ask for broad plugin rewrites without providing versions, build files, and current behavior.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Paper plugins?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Paper APIs, Adventure, MiniMessage, performance patterns, and compatibility with Bukkit or Spigot where needed.

faq.question: Can it generate complete Java plugin code?
faq.answer: It can guide and draft code when the agent supports coding tasks, but this skill file does not include a runnable template.

faq.question: Does it support older Minecraft versions?
faq.answer: It can discuss compatibility strategies, but exact support depends on the target versions, dependencies, and APIs used.

faq.question: Can it help diagnose server lag?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review likely plugin bottlenecks and suggest profiling, async work, caching, and safer event patterns.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: Not always. It recommends web research for current API changes, so users should avoid sharing private details in search queries.

faq.question: Is it only for experienced developers?
faq.answer: No. It can help beginners plan simple plugins and help advanced developers with compatibility, integrations, and performance.
